title: LLMs aren't remotely like compilers or power tools
excerpt: Any metaphor for LLMs that relates them to deterministic systems with discrete interfaces is misleading you.
content_html:
I hear two common refrains in discussions of LLMs:

  • That they're "just the next level of abstraction, like machine code to assembly".
  • That they're "just the next level of automation, like using a table saw or a backhoe".

Both of these statements are wrong to the level of being moronic, and as a result are functioning as thought-terminating cliches (opens new window).

To be clear, I'm not against LLMs on principal, and I think there are ways they can be useful. But the current dialog around them is exhausting and stupid, and I can't stand people making these two particular errors anymore.

§ To use an LLM is to delegate

To best understand why LLMs aren't like compilers or power tools, I want to make clear why delegation is always a better metaphor, and therefore why the other metaphors are so misleading.

When you delegate any amount of work to a person, you can't be sure exactly what you're going to get. A person is a stochastic entity (at least from your perspective), and like any stochastic entity, it can't be precisely controlled in any meaningful way. The Principal-Agent Problem (opens new window) is one of the most difficult and stubborn problems in society.

Humans have many bone-deep evolved intuitions for how to deal with humans, and one of the big ones is to just know you very well might be disappointed! ("If you want something done right, do it yourself.") Delegating work to others and actually getting something useful is extremely hard, despite the fact that humans have general intelligence.

LLMs are profoundly strange by contrast, because they're fundamentally dissimilar to humans, and yet their intrinsic design is to mimic a thing we've only ever experienced humans doing.

A metaphor I've found humorous (but which I'm guessing is disrespectful to addicts and their families... sorry) is that an LLM is like a meth-head who's read the entire internet:

  • They're enthusiastic and work without tire or complaint.
  • They often do work that is acceptable, but not without lots of back-and-forth.
  • If left to their own devices they write wacky code (opens new window).
  • They make bizarre and confounding mistakes (opens new window), like being unable to count words or letters (opens new window).
  • And sometimes they delete the entire production database (opens new window), or expose all your user data or secrets to the internet (opens new window).

Let me be clear: there are situations where I would definitely find it useful (although certainly not ethical...) to delegate some knowledge task to 100 meth-heads (listen to these podcasts and find all the times they talk about type safety; read all US supreme court decisions to find the ones about open source software; third funny example). But I personally think those situations are rare and narrow.

For a time I was tempted to say using an LLM on a project is like outsourcing it to a contractor, but I only think the comparison has any juice if the contractor is pretty bad or has misaligned incentives, or there's a serious language barrier between the groups.

But fundamentally any metaphor will be inadequate, LLMs are too weird. It doesn't make sense to compare a transformer-based deep neural network that autoregressively generates language tokens based on their embedding in a high-dimensional semantic space (opens new window) to really anything that's ever existed.

§ Compilers and power tools are deterministic

Both compilers and power tools are intentionally designed to be completely predictable. The entire point of such tools is that they present a fair amount of automatic power behind the narrowest interface possible. They are machines designed to fully realize the promise of machines, in that they have no will or quirks, you don't have to say thank you (or bully them (opens new window)), and they do exactly the same thing all the time.

  • Power tools package up a large amount of mechanical complexity behind the smallest number of controls possible, e.g. a power button, a control handle, a steering wheel. The intention is that a person can complete repetitive work much faster, work at a much higher level of quality, and hopefully do that work much more safely.
  • Compilers and programming languages are intrinsically about deterministically reusing precise logical structures. All the decisions they made allow you to not have to make those decisions again. Writing reusable software is like you designed some machine part (like a pulley or a gear), but everyone can infinitely copy it and use it precisely the way you designed it.

The interface of an LLM is natural language, and natural language is the most unreliable/fuzzy/imprecise/unpredictable interface to a system possible.

Natural language is the interface of last resort. It's fully general, but it's also furiously ambiguous. Humans fall back to natural language when no other more precise way to describe what we want is available to us, either because it doesn't exist or we don't know it comfortably. We draw diagrams and blueprints and write math equations for a reason!

Backhoes have buttons and joysticks and levers etc because those interfaces make the operation of the machine precise. Computer languages and the GUIs we build with them are constructions of pure logic, meaning we can know exactly what they mean if we do the work to understand them. Natural language could certainly be a smooth onramp (opens new window) to many systems, but once someone knows a discrete interface well, it will always be faster for them to use it than say everything in natural language.

The entire point of using tools is to predictably solve a repetitive problem. LLMs by contrast reproduce a weirder and more confusing version of the principal-agent problem.

Whether you're down for that is up to you, just don't try to convince me you aren't actually delegating.

blocks.html_en: I hear two common refrains in discussions of LLMs:

sentences.en: I hear two common refrains in discussions of LLMs:
sentences.zh: 我在法学硕士的讨论中听到两个常见的说法：

blocks.html_en: • That they're "just the next level of abstraction, like machine code to assembly".

sentences.en: That they're "just the next level of abstraction, like machine code to assembly".
sentences.zh: 它们“只是下一个抽象层次，就像机器代码到汇编”。

blocks.html_en: • That they're "just the next level of automation, like using a table saw or a backhoe".

sentences.en: That they're "just the next level of automation, like using a table saw or a backhoe".
sentences.zh: 它们“只是自动化的下一个级别，就像使用台锯或反铲一样”。

blocks.html_en: Both of these statements are wrong to the level of being moronic, and as a result are functioning as thought-terminating cliches (opens new window).

sentences.en: Both of these statements are wrong to the level of being moronic, and as a result are functioning as thought-terminating cliches (opens new window).
sentences.zh: 这两种说法都是错误的，达到了白痴的程度，因此成为终止思想的陈词滥调（打开新窗口）。

blocks.html_en: To be clear, I'm not against LLMs on principal, and I think there are ways they can be useful. But the current dialog around them is exhausting and stupid, and I can't stand people making these two particular errors anymore.

sentences.en: To be clear, I'm not against LLMs on principal, and I think there are ways they can be useful.
sentences.zh: 需要明确的是，我原则上并不反对法学硕士，而且我认为它们有很多有用的方法。

sentences.en: But the current dialog around them is exhausting and stupid, and I can't stand people making these two particular errors anymore.
sentences.zh: 但目前围绕他们的对话令人筋疲力尽且愚蠢，我无法忍受人们再犯这两个特殊错误。

blocks.html_en: § To use an LLM is to delegate

sentences.en: § To use an LLM is to delegate
sentences.zh: § 使用法学硕士就是委托

blocks.html_en: To best understand why LLMs aren't like compilers or power tools, I want to make clear why delegation is always a better metaphor, and therefore why the other metaphors are so misleading.

sentences.en: To best understand why LLMs aren't like compilers or power tools, I want to make clear why delegation is always a better metaphor, and therefore why the other metaphors are so misleading.
sentences.zh: 为了更好地理解为什么法学硕士不像编译器或电动工具，我想弄清楚为什么委托总是一个更好的比喻，以及为什么其他比喻如此具有误导性。

blocks.html_en: When you delegate any amount of work to a person, you can't be sure exactly what you're going to get. A person is a stochastic entity (at least from your perspective), and like any stochastic entity, it can't be precisely controlled in any meaningful way. The Principal-Agent Problem (opens new window) is one of the most difficult and stubborn problems in society.

sentences.en: When you delegate any amount of work to a person, you can't be sure exactly what you're going to get.
sentences.zh: 当您将任意数量的工作委托给某人时，您无法确定自己到底会得到什么。

sentences.en: A person is a stochastic entity (at least from your perspective), and like any stochastic entity, it can't be precisely controlled in any meaningful way.
sentences.zh: 一个人是一个随机实体（至少从你的角度来看），并且像任何随机实体一样，它不能以任何有意义的方式精确控制。

sentences.en: The Principal-Agent Problem (opens new window) is one of the most difficult and stubborn problems in society.
sentences.zh: 委托代理问题（打开新窗口）是社会上最困难和最顽固的问题之一。

blocks.html_en: Humans have many bone-deep evolved intuitions for how to deal with humans, and one of the big ones is to just know you very well might be disappointed! ("If you want something done right, do it yourself.") Delegating work to others and actually getting something useful is extremely hard, despite the fact that humans have general intelligence.

sentences.en: Humans have many bone-deep evolved intuitions for how to deal with humans, and one of the big ones is to just know you very well might be disappointed!
sentences.zh: 人类对于如何与人打交道有许多深入骨髓的进化直觉，其中最重要的直觉之一就是非常了解你可能会失望！

sentences.en: ("If you want something done right, do it yourself.") Delegating work to others and actually getting something useful is extremely hard, despite the fact that humans have general intelligence.
sentences.zh: （“如果你想把某件事做好，那就自己做。”）尽管人类具有一般智力，但将工作委托给他人并真正获得有用的东西是极其困难的。

blocks.html_en: LLMs are profoundly strange by contrast, because they're fundamentally dissimilar to humans, and yet their intrinsic design is to mimic a thing we've only ever experienced humans doing.

sentences.en: LLMs are profoundly strange by contrast, because they're fundamentally dissimilar to humans, and yet their intrinsic design is to mimic a thing we've only ever experienced humans doing.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，法学硕士就非常奇怪了，因为它们从根本上与人类不同，但它们的内在设计是模仿我们只经历过人类做过的事情。

sentences.en: A metaphor I've found humorous (but which I'm guessing is disrespectful to addicts and their families...
sentences.zh: 我觉得这个比喻很幽默（但我猜这是对吸毒者及其家人的不尊重......

sentences.en: sorry) is that an LLM is like a meth-head who's read the entire internet:
sentences.zh: 抱歉）法学硕士就像一个读过整个互联网的吸毒者：

blocks.html_en: • They're enthusiastic and work without tire or complaint.

sentences.en: They're enthusiastic and work without tire or complaint.
sentences.zh: 他们工作热情高涨，毫无疲倦，毫无怨言。

blocks.html_en: • They often do work that is acceptable, but not without lots of back-and-forth.

sentences.en: They often do work that is acceptable, but not without lots of back-and-forth.
sentences.zh: 他们所做的工作常常是可以接受的，但并非没有大量的反复。

blocks.html_en: • If left to their own devices they write wacky code (opens new window).

sentences.en: If left to their own devices they write wacky code (opens new window).
sentences.zh: 如果留给他们自己的设备，他们会编写古怪的代码（打开新窗口）。

blocks.html_en: • They make bizarre and confounding mistakes (opens new window), like being unable to count words or letters (opens new window).

sentences.en: They make bizarre and confounding mistakes (opens new window), like being unable to count words or letters (opens new window).
sentences.zh: 他们会犯一些奇怪且令人困惑的错误（打开新窗口），例如无法计算单词或字母（打开新窗口）。

blocks.html_en: • And sometimes they delete the entire production database (opens new window), or expose all your user data or secrets to the internet (opens new window).

sentences.en: And sometimes they delete the entire production database (opens new window), or expose all your user data or secrets to the internet (opens new window).
sentences.zh: 有时他们会删除整个生产数据库（打开新窗口），或将您的所有用户数据或秘密暴露到互联网（打开新窗口）。

blocks.html_en: Let me be clear: there are situations where I would definitely find it useful (although certainly not ethical...) to delegate some knowledge task to 100 meth-heads (listen to these podcasts and find all the times they talk about type safety; read all US supreme court decisions to find the ones about open source software; third funny example). But I personally think those situations are rare and narrow.

sentences.en: Let me be clear: there are situations where I would definitely find it useful (although certainly not ethical...)
sentences.zh: 让我明确一点：在某些情况下我肯定会发现它有用（尽管肯定不道德......）

sentences.en: to delegate some knowledge task to 100 meth-heads (listen to these podcasts and find all the times they talk about type safety; read all US supreme court decisions to find the ones about open source software; third funny example).
sentences.zh: 将一些知识任务委托给 100 个吸毒者（听这些播客，发现他们每次都在谈论类型安全；阅读所有美国最高法院的判决，找到有关开源软件的判决；第三个有趣的例子）。

sentences.en: But I personally think those situations are rare and narrow.
sentences.zh: 但我个人认为这种情况很少见，而且范围很窄。

blocks.html_en: For a time I was tempted to say using an LLM on a project is like outsourcing it to a contractor, but I only think the comparison has any juice if the contractor is pretty bad or has misaligned incentives, or there's a serious language barrier between the groups.

sentences.en: For a time I was tempted to say using an LLM on a project is like outsourcing it to a contractor, but I only think the comparison has any juice if the contractor is pretty bad or has misaligned incentives, or there's a serious language barrier between the groups.
sentences.zh: 有一段时间，我很想说在一个项目上使用法学硕士就像将其外包给承包商一样，但我认为只有当承包商非常糟糕或激励措施不一致，或者团队之间存在严重的语言障碍时，这种比较才有意义。

blocks.html_en: But fundamentally any metaphor will be inadequate, LLMs are too weird. It doesn't make sense to compare a transformer-based deep neural network that autoregressively generates language tokens based on their embedding in a high-dimensional semantic space (opens new window) to really anything that's ever existed.

sentences.en: But fundamentally any metaphor will be inadequate, LLMs are too weird.
sentences.zh: 但从根本上讲，任何比喻都是不够的，法学硕士太奇怪了。

sentences.en: It doesn't make sense to compare a transformer-based deep neural network that autoregressively generates language tokens based on their embedding in a high-dimensional semantic space (opens new window) to really anything that's ever existed.
sentences.zh: 将基于变压器的深度神经网络（基于高维语义空间（打开新窗口）的嵌入自回归生成语言标记）与任何曾经存在的东西进行比较是没有意义的。

blocks.html_en: § Compilers and power tools are deterministic

sentences.en: Computer languages and the GUIs we build with them are constructions of pure logic, meaning we can know exactly what they mean if we do the work to understand them.
sentences.zh: 计算机语言和我们用它们构建的 GUI 是纯逻辑的构造，这意味着如果我们努力理解它们，我们就可以准确地知道它们的含义。

sentences.en: Natural language could certainly be a smooth onramp (opens new window) to many systems, but once someone knows a discrete interface well, it will always be faster for them to use it than say everything in natural language.

blocks.html_en: The entire point of using tools is to predictably solve a repetitive problem. LLMs by contrast reproduce a weirder and more confusing version of the principal-agent problem.

blocks.html_en: Whether you're down for that is up to you, just don't try to convince me you aren't actually delegating.

sentences.en: Whether you're down for that is up to you, just don't try to convince me you aren't actually delegating.
sentences.zh: 无论你是否对此感到失望取决于你自己，只是不要试图说服我你实际上并没有在授权。

content_html_zh:
我在法学硕士的讨论中听到两个常见的说法：

  • 它们“只是下一个抽象层次，就像机器代码到汇编”。
  • 它们“只是自动化的下一个级别，就像使用台锯或反铲一样”。

这两种说法都是错误的，达到了白痴的程度，因此成为终止思想的陈词滥调（打开新窗口）。

需要明确的是，我原则上并不反对法学硕士，而且我认为它们有很多有用的方法。 但目前围绕他们的对话令人筋疲力尽且愚蠢，我无法忍受人们再犯这两个特殊错误。

§ 使用法学硕士就是委托

为了更好地理解为什么法学硕士不像编译器或电动工具，我想弄清楚为什么委托总是一个更好的比喻，以及为什么其他比喻如此具有误导性。

当您将任意数量的工作委托给某人时，您无法确定自己到底会得到什么。 一个人是一个随机实体（至少从你的角度来看），并且像任何随机实体一样，它不能以任何有意义的方式精确控制。 委托代理问题（打开新窗口）是社会上最困难和最顽固的问题之一。

人类对于如何与人打交道有许多深入骨髓的进化直觉，其中最重要的直觉之一就是非常了解你可能会失望！ （“如果你想把某件事做好，那就自己做。”）尽管人类具有一般智力，但将工作委托给他人并真正获得有用的东西是极其困难的。

相比之下，法学硕士就非常奇怪了，因为它们从根本上与人类不同，但它们的内在设计是模仿我们只经历过人类做过的事情。

我觉得这个比喻很幽默（但我猜这是对吸毒者及其家人的不尊重...... 抱歉）法学硕士就像一个读过整个互联网的吸毒者：

  • 他们工作热情高涨，毫无疲倦，毫无怨言。
  • 他们所做的工作常常是可以接受的，但并非没有大量的反复。
  • 如果留给他们自己的设备，他们会编写古怪的代码（打开新窗口）。
  • 他们会犯一些奇怪且令人困惑的错误（打开新窗口），例如无法计算单词或字母（打开新窗口）。
  • 有时他们会删除整个生产数据库（打开新窗口），或将您的所有用户数据或秘密暴露到互联网（打开新窗口）。

让我明确一点：在某些情况下我肯定会发现它有用（尽管肯定不道德......） 将一些知识任务委托给 100 个吸毒者（听这些播客，发现他们每次都在谈论类型安全；阅读所有美国最高法院的判决，找到有关开源软件的判决；第三个有趣的例子）。 但我个人认为这种情况很少见，而且范围很窄。

有一段时间，我很想说在一个项目上使用法学硕士就像将其外包给承包商一样，但我认为只有当承包商非常糟糕或激励措施不一致，或者团队之间存在严重的语言障碍时，这种比较才有意义。

但从根本上讲，任何比喻都是不够的，法学硕士太奇怪了。 将基于变压器的深度神经网络（基于高维语义空间（打开新窗口）的嵌入自回归生成语言标记）与任何曾经存在的东西进行比较是没有意义的。

§ 编译器和电动工具是确定性的

编译器和电动工具都被有意设计为完全可预测的。 此类工具的全部要点在于，它们在尽可能窄的接口背后提供了相当多的自动功能。 它们是为完全实现机器承诺而设计的机器，因为它们没有意志或怪癖，你不必说谢谢（或欺负它们（打开新窗口）），而且它们一直在做完全相同的事情。

  • 电动工具将大量的机械复杂性封装在尽可能少的控制装置后面，例如电源按钮、控制手柄、方向盘。 目的是让一个人可以更快地完成重复性工作，以更高的质量水平工作，并希望更安全地完成这项工作。
  • 编译器和编程语言本质上是确定性地重用精确的逻辑结构。 他们做出的所有决定让您不必再次做出这些决定。 编写可重用软件就像您设计了一些机器零件（例如滑轮或齿轮），但每个人都可以无限地复制它并按照您设计的方式精确使用它。

LLM 的界面是自然语言，而自然语言是系统可能的最不可靠/模糊/不精确/不可预测的界面。

自然语言是最后手段的界面。 它非常笼统，但也极其模糊。 当没有其他更精确的方式来描述我们想要的东西时，人类就会回归自然语言，要么因为它不存在，要么我们不舒服地知道它。 我们画图表、蓝图、写数学方程都是有原因的！

反铲挖土机有按钮、操纵杆和控制杆等，因为这些接口使机器的操作更加精确。 计算机语言和我们用它们构建的 GUI 是纯逻辑的构造，这意味着如果我们努力理解它们，我们就可以准确地知道它们的含义。 自然语言当然可以成为许多系统的平滑入口（打开新窗口），但是一旦有人很好地了解离散接口，他们使用它总是比用自然语言说出所有内容更快。

使用工具的全部意义在于以可预测的方式解决重复性问题。 相比之下，法学硕士重现了委托代理问题的一个更奇怪、更令人困惑的版本。

无论你是否对此感到失望取决于你自己，只是不要试图说服我你实际上并没有在授权。
